role_title: Program Manager, Revenue Strategy and Operations

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and one flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

level_signals: 4+ years of relevant experience in project or program management or sales/success teams
Comp range $116K-$159.5K base, consistent with mid-senior IC at a growth-stage tech company
Cross-functional leadership without explicit people management mentioned

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years of relevant experience project or program management or working on a success/sales team
You're a strategic architect and have a product management mindset – you are able to think broadly about long-term solutions to problems that improve the experience for all teams
You're a cross-functional guru – you can communicate with teams across the organization (Sales, Success, S&A, Enablement, etc.) to understand stakeholder needs and translate them into solutions
You're a clear communicator — you are effective at making the complex simple - and in specific technical detail when needed to ensure we build through the detail
You're able to project manage and prioritize resources to execute against a deliverable, and you are able to assign work effectively to partners and follow through to execution
You are able to influence and architect priorities across the team you are supporting
Are a systems and process expert: Salesforce, Salesloft, Orum, Gong and understand how these tools can be best utilized by the team, continuing to improve upon them
You're relentlessly resourceful - Won't quit on a problem because the solution is not obvious
You're passionate about serving our community - you believe in Faire's mission to help local retailers and independent brands succeed

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: are growing into communication with external stakeholders (Product, Marketing, Engineering, or Data Science)
test and drive adoption of the Unify outreach tool
partnering with Data science on scoring and scraping projects

scope.surface: Retailer Sales Functions — program management for Mid Market Top of Funnel, Book Build infrastructure, email & call strategy, quarterly initiative roadmaps
scope.business_model: B2B wholesale marketplace (technology platform connecting independent retailers and brands)
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption rates of sales programs and tools
Conversion improvements across the sales funnel
core_responsibilities: Translate business needs into clear requirements for Revenue Systems and other partners
Partner with Revenue Management on initiative prioritization and with S&A to scope strategies
Ensure individual contributors have tools, workflows, and support to operate effectively
Build quarterly initiative roadmaps with long-term goals
Drive Mid Market Top of Funnel strategy including Book Build infrastructure and email & call strategy
Identify and resolve process inefficiencies in systems and workflows
Maintain and improve Salesforce Homebase hub and related tooling
Drive cross-functional alignment on quarterly roadmap and flag risks and dependencies

green_flags: Equity mentioned as part of compensation
Clear scope: Retailer Sales program management with specific tool and process areas named
Specific tools named (Salesforce, Salesloft, Gong, Orum) signal a concrete, operational role
Hybrid policy clearly defined (3 days/week in office, 4 weeks remote flexibility per year)
Well-known investors and founders (Square alumni, Y Combinator, Sequoia) signal company stability
red_flags: Role is San Francisco-based hybrid only — no remote option stated beyond the 4-week annual flexibility
Work authorization and visa sponsorship status not addressed in the JD
Qualifications section mixes hard skills with cultural/personality traits, making it harder to assess true bar
No explicit people management but scope of influence across global revenue org is broad for a non-manager

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not directly stated)
ambiguities: The Qualifications section does not use 'Required' vs 'Preferred' language explicitly; all bullets are treated as must_haves except items framed as growth areas ('growing into communication with external stakeholders') or tool-specific examples that appear secondary to the core requirements (Unify tool, Data Science collaboration), which are classified as nice_to_haves.

The JD mentions 'Global Program Management Support' but the salary is only listed for San Francisco, CA — it is unclear whether this role is open in other Faire office locations (Toronto, London, New York, Kitchener-Waterloo).
